free_text: High-precision 144Sm(a,a)144Sm scattering at low
energies and the rate of the 144Sm(a,g)148Gd reaction
Targets were made by reductive vacuum evaporation of
Sm2O3 powder enriched to 96.5% in 144Sm onto 40 ug/cm2
thick carbon foils. The target thicknesses were found
to be 150 ug/cm2 with an uncertainty of 9%.
Seven ion-implanted silicon detectors with active
areas of 50 mm2 were used for the measurement of the
angular distributions. The detectors were collimated
with d = 1 mm wide slits and were mounted on two
turntables. Two detectors with angular separation of
10 degree were mounted on the upper turntable at
forward angles. Five detectors were placed on the lower
turntable, with angular separation of was 5 degree.
The solid angles were typically between 0.97E-04 and
Two additional detectors were mounted at fixed angles
+- 15degree with respect to the beam direction. These
monitor detectors were used to normalize the measured
angular distributions and to determine the precise
position of the beam on the target. The solid angle of
the detectors was 8.1E-06 sr.
The horizontal size of the beam spot was below 2 mm
which is crucial for the precise determination of the
scattering angle.
Quadrature sum of contribution from the
angular position uncertainty and
statistical uncertainty (0.1% - 4%)
